labels_by_row.66: I assume that reinvestment in a year translates into growth in the next year, i.e., there is a one year lag between reinvesting and generating growth from that reinvestment.
labels_by_row.67: Do you want override this assumption =
labels_by_row.68: If yes, enter a different lag (0 = no lag to 3 = lag of 3 years)
labels_by_row.69: I will assume that your effective tax rate will adjust to your marginal tax rate by your terminal year. If you override this assumption, I will leave the tax rate at your effective tax rate.

labels_by_row.74: I will asssume that today's risk free rate will prevail in perpetuity. If you override this assumption, I will change the riskfree rate after year 10.
labels_by_row.75: Do you want to override this assumption =
labels_by_row.76: If yes, enter the riskfree rate after year 10
labels_by_row.77: I will assume that the growth rate in perpetuity will be equal to the risk free rate. This allows for both valuation consistency and prevents "impossible" growth rates.
labels_by_row.78: Do you want to override this assumption =

labels_by_row.2: The Opportunistic Chip Maker, after a DeepSeek Disruption
labels_by_row.3: NVIDIA has a history of innovation and opportunism in the chip business, often investing ahead of large trends in demand in crypto and gaming and now in AI and auto. Its early entry into the AI chip business will allow it to dominate this business, but the DeepSeek entry has disrupted the market, creating a two tier market - one commoditized and the other premier; Nvidia will dominate teh latter.. In the auto chip business, NVidia will grow to gain a significant, albeit not dominant, market share as that market grows. Across all of its busineses, NVIDIA will see sustained high operating margins, though the makers of Nvidia chips (especially TSMC) and TSMC's big customers (big tech) will push back, causing margins to drop from 2024 levels.

cells.value: Tell your story about the company. Keep it focuses on the company's businesses and tie it into the three key levers of value: cash flows, growth and risk
cells.cached_value: Tell your story about the company. Keep it focuses on the company's businesses and tie it into the three key levers of value: cash flows, growth and risk
cells.row_label: NVIDIA has a history of innovation and opportunism in the chip business, often investing ahead of large trends in demand in crypto and gaming and now in AI and auto. Its early entry into the AI chip business will allow it to dominate this business, but the DeepSeek entry has disrupted the market, creating a two tier market - one commoditized and the other premier; Nvidia will dominate teh latter.. In the auto chip business, NVidia will grow to gain a significant, albeit not dominant, market share as that market grows. Across all of its busineses, NVIDIA will see sustained high operating margins, though the makers of Nvidia chips (especially TSMC) and TSMC's big customers (big tech) will push back, causing margins to drop from 2024 levels.

cells.value: The AI business will be big, but the DeepSeek entry has bifurcated it, into commoditized and premium, and Nvidia will continue to maintain its dominance of the latter.
cells.cached_value: The AI business will be big, but the DeepSeek entry has bifurcated it, into commoditized and premium, and Nvidia will continue to maintain its dominance of the latter.

cells.value: Nvidia will continue to have high margins in its core businesses, and while the AI chip market will be smaller than it was pre-DeepSeek, but some of Nvidia's current competitors may shift to the other AI market.

cells.value: Until 2019, this worksheet had to be used for every firm with operating lease commitments, since accountants treated operating lease expenses as operating expenses. In 2019, IFRS and GAAP made the long-overdue change to treat leases as debt, and for firms that follow these standards, the conversion that I am doing on this spreadsheet is being done by accountants, with lease debt and operating income already adjusted. If that is the case, and you showed the lease debt as part of debt in the input page, please do not use this spreadsheet, since you will be double counting.

If that is the case, and you showed the lease debt as part of debt in the input page, please do not use this spreadsheet, since you will be double counting.

labels_by_row.1: You can use this spreadsheet to compute your cost of capital. You can either use the built-in data to compute key inputs (beta, equity risk premium, default spread) or enter them directly. If you choose to use the built in ERP data, you can update the ERP numbers to reflect a more current mature market premuum (since the spreadsheet uses the start of the year numbers).
labels_by_row.3: Estimation of Current Cost of Capital
labels_by_row.4: There are four ways in whch you can estimate your cost of capital. In the first, you can directly input a cost of captial. In the second, you can work through your company's cost of capital inputs in detail, entering business, geogaphical mix and debt, and I will help you estimate a cost of capital. In the third, you can look up the cost of capital for the industry your firm belongs to and in the fourth, you can use a crosssectoinal distribution  of costs of capital for companies at the start of the year to make your estimate.

cells.row_label: Estimation of Current Cost of Capital

There are four ways in whch you can estimate your cost of capital. In the first, you can directly input a cost of captial. In the second, you can work through your company's cost of capital inputs in detail, entering business, geogaphical mix and debt, and I will help you estimate a cost of capital. In the third, you can look up the cost of capital for the industry your firm belongs to and in the fourth, you can use a crosssectoinal distribution  of costs of capital for companies at the start of the year to make your estimate.

cells.value: The last two rows in each of country/region risk premium tables is set aside for your input to provide you with flexibility to enter some numbers directly. For instance, assume that you have a company that breaks its revenues down into three countries and then puts the rest into "Rest of the World". You can enter the "Rest of the World" in one of these two rows and enter an equity risk premium for the rest of the world. The easiest way to do that is to go into the country equity risk premium worksheet and change the GDP for the three countries that you have data for to zero and compute the global weighted average ERP for the remaining countries. With the regional worksheet, you can use the last two rows to enter the data for an individual country (usually the domestic country) that might be broken out though the rest of the revenues are broken down by region. You can look up the ERP for the country in the country ERP worksheet.

With the regional worksheet, you can use the last two rows to enter the data for an individual country (usually the domestic country) that might be broken out though the rest of the revenues are broken down by region. You can look up the ERP for the country in the country ERP worksheet.

labels_by_row.2: Approach 1: Using a corporate bond rating
labels_by_row.4: Default Probabilities over time (1 - 10 year time horizons)
labels_by_row.14: Thus, if you use the 10-year likelihood of failure, the chance of failure for a BB rated firm is 11.78%.
labels_by_row.16: Approach 2: Using corporate age (for young companies)
labels_by_row.31: Thus, if you are valuing a technology firm that is 6 years old, the chance of failure is 11.70%.
labels_by_row.34: 1. The likelihood of failure decreases for larger firms.
labels_by_row.35: 2. The likelihood of failure decreases with access to capital, and is thus lower for publicly traded firms or firms with multiple high profile VCs
labels_by_row.36: 3. The likelihood of failure decreases as revenue growth increases, since hope for future growth will sustain the firm.
labels_by_row.37: 4. The likelihood of failure decreases with better unit economics, higher gross margins on the additional units sold
